entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The crop studied, Arachis hypogaea L., produced in Zambia

entities.name: ZAMBIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted

entities.name: CHONGWE DISTRICT
entities.description: The specific district in Zambia where the field experiment took place

entities.name: COMPOST MANURE
entities.description: Soil amendment derived from composted cattle manure used to improve soil properties

entities.name: SOIL MICROBIAL RESPIRATION
entities.description: A measure of soil microbial activity affected by compost manure

entities.name: PLANT-AVAILABLE WATER
entities.description: The amount of water available for plant uptake in the soil, also known as PAW

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of peanut kernels and pods

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: Pre-harvest contamination of peanut kernels by aflatoxins

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENT
entities.description: The research method involving rain-fed cropping seasons to evaluate compost effects

relationships.description: Positive association with increase in pod and kernel yield
Reduction in total aflatoxin levels with increasing levels of compost (R2=0.85)
Improvement in soil moisture retention capacity contributes to increased peanut yield
Improvement in soil microbial activity contributes to increased peanut yield
Improvement in soil moisture retention capacity contributes to decreased aflatoxin concentrations
Improvement in soil microbial activity contributes to decreased aflatoxin concentrations
Yield is the measured productivity of the peanut crop
Aflatoxin contamination occurs in the kernels of the peanut crop
The experiment was conducted to evaluate effects on peanut production
The experiment was conducted in Zambia
Chongwe District is located within Zambia
